---
title: "Methods & technical design"
subtitle: "How raw satellite imagery becomes a validated damaged-cropland hectare"
---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**In one line.** Two independent remote-sensing pipelines — Sentinel-1 SAR for floods,
Sentinel-2 + VIIRS for fires — are forced through **one shared schema** and **one human
validation gate**, so every number in this report is a human-checked, reproducible
`damaged_cropland_ha`. This page is the engineering behind the headlines.
:::

The credibility of a conflict-zone damage estimate rests on *how* it was produced. This
report's spine is a deliberate piece of design: two phenomena, two pipelines, but a
**single output contract** and a **single point where a human signs off**. Nothing
downstream can see a pipeline's internal rasters; it sees only validated records.

## The integration contract

Floods and fires are measured by completely different physics — radar backscatter vs
optical burn spectra — yet both pipelines emit the *same* record: `damaged_cropland_ha`
per area-of-interest (AOI) per date, tagged with phenomenon, severity, and a validation
status. That shared schema (`schema/damage_schema.py`) is what makes this **one study
rather than two**: the food-security layer and the three research questions read the
schema and nothing else.

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-architecture
%%| fig-cap: "The dual-pipeline architecture. Two sensors, two server-side builders, one local authoritative hectare, one schema, one human gate — then the food-security and research-question layers, which never touch pipeline internals."
flowchart TD
  S1[Sentinel-1 SAR] --> FM[flood_mask<br/>dual-pol AND + HAND gate]
  S2[Sentinel-2 optical] --> BS[burn_severity<br/>dNBR bins + VIIRS confirm]
  VI[VIIRS 375 m active fire] --> BS
  CM[WorldCover + Dynamic World] --> MASK[cropland_mask.tif<br/>human-reviewed]
  FM --> HA[damaged_cropland_ha<br/>union vs intersection]
  BS --> HA
  MASK --> HA
  HA --> SCHEMA[Shared damage schema]
  SCHEMA --> GATE{Tier-2 human<br/>validation gate}
  GATE -->|validated only| FS[Food-security impact]
  GATE -->|validated only| RQ[RQ1 / RQ2 / RQ3]
  CH[CHIRPS rainfall] --> RQ
  GF[GloFAS discharge] --> RQ
```

## Compute on the cloud, decide locally

The work happens in two places, and the split is the point:

- **Google Earth Engine (server-side).** All petabyte-scale imagery lives in the GEE
  catalog; we never download a scene. Each pipeline builds a *lazy* `ee.Image`
  computation graph — filter → mask → arithmetic → threshold — that GEE evaluates only
  when we ask for a number or a bounded raster tile.
- **Local (the authoritative numbers).** The hectares that actually enter the report are
  recomputed **on-machine against the human-reviewed `aois/cropland_mask.tif`**, never a
  server-side proxy. Speckle cleanup, clipping, and the validation packets are local.

> Rule of thumb: GEE does the heavy raster algebra; the *trusted* numbers are recomputed
> locally against committed, human-reviewed artifacts — the ones a person gates.

## The sensors

Each input is chosen for one job, and each carries a documented limitation that shapes
how its output is used.

::: {.method-table}
| Source (GEE / API) | Sensor / type | Role — and its honest limit |
|---|---|---|
| `COPERNICUS/S1_GRD` | Sentinel-1 C-band SAR (VV+VH) | **Flood extent** — sees water through cloud; captures *open standing water only* (flooded vegetation under-detected) |
| `COPERNICUS/S2_SR_HARMONIZED` | Sentinel-2 optical (B8, B12) | **Burn severity** via dNBR; over cropland a burn scar is spectrally indistinguishable from harvest/ploughing |
| FIRMS **VIIRS 375 m** | Active-fire thermal | **Fire confirmation** — the thermal proof a dNBR scar was fire, not harvest (never MODIS for detection) |
| `ESA/WorldCover/v200` | 10 m land cover | Cropland mask — *liberal* source (rainfed/fallow) |
| `GOOGLE/DYNAMICWORLD/V1` | 10 m near-real-time | Cropland mask — *conservative* source (`crops`>0.35) |
| `JRC/GSW1_4/GlobalSurfaceWater` | Permanent-water occurrence | Subtract the permanent river so it is never counted as damage |
| `MERIT/Hydro/v1_0_1` (HAND) | Height-above-drainage | Floodplain gate — rejects dry upland radar false positives |
| `COPERNICUS/DEM/GLO30` | 30 m DEM → slope | Rejects radar-shadow false positives |
| `UCSB-CHG/CHIRPS/DAILY` | Gridded rainfall (~5.5 km) | Drought baseline + RQ1 local-rainfall check |
| GloFAS (`cems-glofas-*`) | River-discharge reanalysis | RQ1 riverine attribution (reanalysis, not a gauge) |
:::

Every GEE collection ID was verified against the live catalog before use; one drift was
caught and flagged (CHIRPS daily resolves at `UCSB-CHG/CHIRPS/DAILY`, not the spec's
older slash form) rather than silently patched.

## How an image becomes a hectare

**Floods — a change in radar backscatter.** Smooth open water reflects radar away from
the sensor, so it reads dark; a flood is a *drop* versus a no-flood reference. The chain
guards against the two ways that fools you:

1. **In-season, per-relative-orbit reference.** The backdrop is the median of all
   March–June acquisitions *of the same orbit* as the event. *In-season* removes
   crop-phenology bias (an early dry-summer reference flagged dense spring canopy as
   "flood" across whole governorates); *per-orbit* keeps the incidence-angle geometry
   matched.
2. **Dual-pol AND, not OR.** `flood = (VV drop ≥ 4 dB) AND (VV < −18 dB) AND (VH < −24
   dB)` — requiring *both* bands to be water-dark roughly halves false positives.
3. **HAND floodplain gate (< 15 m).** Post-harvest bare fields are also radar-dark; the
   height-above-drainage gate keeps low-lying riverine flood and drops dry uplands. Then
   permanent water (JRC) and steep slopes (GLO-30) are subtracted.

**Fires — a spectral scar, thermally confirmed.** `NBR = (B8 − B12)/(B8 + B12)` on
cloud-masked Sentinel-2 medians; `dNBR = NBR_pre − NBR_post` rises over burns, binned to
severity at the USGS/Key-&-Benson thresholds (0.10 / 0.27 / 0.44 / 0.66). Because dNBR
*also* drops at harvest, a scar counts as fire damage **only within 375 m of a VIIRS
active-fire detection** — the thermal discriminator. This is conservative by design (a
VIIRS-missed burn is not counted), so burned hectares are a lower bound.

**Cropland, reported as a range.** The two best 10 m cropland sources disagree, so rather
than pick one, the mask encodes both *and their disagreement* in one categorical raster:
union (either source) is the headline, intersection (both agree) the conservative low
bound. **Every damage figure in this report is therefore a union-vs-intersection range,**
the honest expression of cropland-definition uncertainty.

## The validation framework

Correctness here is not "the tests pass." It is two distinct tiers:

- **Tier 1 — agent-verifiable.** Schema conformance, gate logic, caching, arithmetic —
  covered by **85 automated tests**. A machine can certify these.
- **Tier 2 — human-gated.** Flood masks, burn scars, and any `damaged_cropland_ha`
  derived from them are validated by a human domain expert against named ground truth
  (GloFAS event windows, Copernicus EMS). **No code path can set a record to
  `validated`** — the food-security layer *refuses* (raises) if handed a single
  non-validated record. The gate is enforced in code via `viz.consumable_records()`.

The panels below are exactly what a human reviewed to close each gate — VIIRS hotspots
over post-fire Sentinel-2 with the dNBR severity overlay (fires), and per-pixel flood
frequency, transient vs persistent (floods). They are the *evidence behind the gate*,
shown so a reader can inspect it too.

```{python}
#| label: validation-gallery
#| echo: false
# The validation panels live in outputs/ (the data product), outside this Quarto
# project dir, so Quarto would not publish a static `../outputs/...` reference.
# Read each committed PNG, downscale it to a lightweight JPEG thumbnail, and embed
# it as a base64 data-URI in a self-contained CSS grid (.gallery-grid in
# styles.css) — canonical file, no duplication, ~1.7 MB for the whole gallery.
import io, base64, pathlib
from PIL import Image
from IPython.display import HTML

REPO_ROOT = None
for _cand in (pathlib.Path.cwd(), *pathlib.Path.cwd().parents):
    if (_cand / "CLAUDE.md").exists() and (_cand / "outputs").is_dir():
        REPO_ROOT = _cand
        break
OUT = REPO_ROOT / "outputs"

def _uri(path, width=820, quality=78):
    """Committed PNG -> downscaled JPEG data-URI (satellite imagery is photographic,
    so JPEG is ~20x smaller than the source PNG at display resolution)."""
    im = Image.open(path).convert("RGB")
    w, h = im.size
    if w > width:
        im = im.resize((width, round(h * width / w)), Image.LANCZOS)
    buf = io.BytesIO()
    im.save(buf, format="JPEG", quality=quality, optimize=True)
    return "data:image/jpeg;base64," + base64.b64encode(buf.getvalue()).decode()

def _grid(panels):
    items = "".join(
        f'<figure class="gallery-item"><img loading="lazy" src="{_uri(p)}" alt="{c}">'
        f'<figcaption>{c}</figcaption></figure>'
        for p, c in panels
    )
    return f'<div class="gallery-grid">{items}</div>'

FV = OUT / "fire_validation"
FF = OUT / "floods" / "validation_packet"

fire = [
    (FV / "hasakah_2026_validation.png", "Hasakah — the core 2026 cropland-fire signal"),
    (FV / "raqqa_2026_validation.png", "Raqqa — large scars, few hotspots"),
    (FV / "deir_ez_zor_2026_validation.png", "Deir ez-Zor — hotspots mostly off-cropland (discriminator working)"),
    (FV / "idlib_2026_validation.png", "Idlib"),
    (FV / "aleppo_2026_validation.png", "Aleppo"),
    (FV / "hama_2026_validation.png", "Hama"),
    (FV / "homs_2026_validation.png", "Homs"),
    (FV / "daraa_2026_validation.png", "Daraa"),
    (FV / "rural_damascus_2026_validation.png", "Rural Damascus"),
    (FV / "quneitra_2026_validation.png", "Quneitra"),
    (FV / "suwayda_2026_validation.png", "Suwayda"),
    (FV / "tartus_2026_validation.png", "Tartus"),
]
anchor_flood = [
    (FV / "latakia_2025_emsr811_validation.png", "Latakia 2025 — EMSR811 method-anchor (baseline, not a study record)"),
    (FF / "deir_ez_zor_flood_frequency.png", "Deir ez-Zor — flood frequency (transient vs persistent)"),
    (FF / "raqqa_flood_frequency.png", "Raqqa — flood frequency"),
    (FF / "hasakah_flood_frequency.png", "Hasakah — flood frequency (June extent flagged, see RQ1)"),
]
note = (
    '<p class="gallery-note">Fire validation panels, 2026 study window. '
    '<span class="anchor-note">The 2025 Latakia EMSR811 panel below is a method-anchor '
    '— a baseline-year demonstration against a Copernicus EMS forest fire, not a study '
    'damage record (DEC-001).</span></p>'
)
HTML(_grid(fire) + note + _grid(anchor_flood))
```

The cropland mask itself was the first artifact gated, reviewed per-AOI for its
union / intersection / disagreement structure before any pipeline consumed it:

```{python}
#| label: cropland-qc
#| echo: false
HTML(
    '<figure class="fig-frame" style="max-width:70%;margin:0 auto;">'
    f'<img src="{_uri(OUT / "aoi_qc" / "deir_ez_zor_cropland_mask.png")}" '
    'alt="Cropland-mask QC for Deir ez-Zor: union, intersection, and source disagreement.">'
    '<figcaption style="font-size:0.8em;color:#555;margin-top:0.3rem;">'
    'Cropland-mask QC, Deir ez-Zor: union, intersection, and where the two source '
    'satellites disagree (largest spread, ~72%, in this rainfed governorate).'
    '</figcaption></figure>'
)
```

## Reproducibility by design

The whole study is built to reproduce from a clean checkout without a human in the loop
*except* at the Tier-2 gate:

- **Non-interactive GEE auth.** A service-account key path was added because Google
  blocks the legacy `drive` OAuth scope for the default client — so the pipeline
  authenticates headless, no browser step.
- **Cached, checkpointed pulls.** One shared cache backs all four external clients
  (FIRMS, CHIRPS, ACLED, GloFAS); a cache hit *never* calls the network, so retries and
  resumes spend no quota and a multi-day pull resumes mid-stream.
- **Frozen presentation.** This Quarto site renders with `freeze: auto` and reads only
  committed, validated CSV tables — so publishing **never re-triggers an expensive GEE
  pull**, and the figures are byte-stable. The build needs no geospatial stack at all.
- **Static artifact.** A version-controlled site (no server, no database) was chosen over
  a dashboard precisely because reproducibility is a first-class goal here, not an
  operational live tool.

## Honest limitations

Rigor includes saying what the numbers are *not*:

- **First-half-2026 lower bounds.** Windows cover floods Mar–Jun and fires May 1 – Jun 12;
  the summer harvest and fire peak are unobserved. The study is built to re-run
  post-harvest for the concluded result.
- **RQ2 data gap.** Live ACLED Syria coverage ends 2025-06-13, so the 2026 fire–conflict
  overlay cannot yet be computed; the method is demonstrated on the fully-covered 2025
  window and will complete automatically once 2026 data is ingested.
- **One flagged record.** Hasakah's June flood hectares match a known SAR
  harvest-artifact failure mode and are flagged, not silently dropped; the food-security
  headline is robust to them (it moves ~5%).
- **Field verification is the gold standard.** Remote-sensing self-consistency is not
  ground truth; expert and field confirmation sit above it, and the design invites that
  cross-check rather than foreclosing it.

{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
